author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXII. 1-14.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus answered and spake againin parables.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The kingdom of Heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriagefor his son; and sent forth his servants to call them which were bidden to the wedding; and they would not come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, I have prepared my dinner; my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready; come unto the marriage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his farm, another to his merchandise: and the remnant took his servants, and entreated them spitefully, and slew them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou both in the former parable and in this the difference between the Son and the servants?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou at once the great affinity between both parables, and the great difference also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this also indicates God’s long-suffering, and His great providential care, and the Jews’ ingratitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this parable hath something also more than the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it proclaims beforehand both the casting out of the Jews, and the calling of the Gentiles; and it indicates together with this also the strictness of the life required, and how great the punishment appointed for the careless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And well is this placed after the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He had said, “It shall be given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof,” He declares next to what kind of nation; and not this only, but He also again sets forth His providential care towards the Jews as past utterance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there He appears before His crucifixion bidding them; but here even after He is slain, He still urges them, striving to win them over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they deserved to have suffered the most grievous punishment, then He both presses them to the marriage, and honors them with the highest honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how both there He calls not the Gentiles first, but the Jews, and here again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as there, when they would not receive Him, but even slew Him when He was come, then He gave away the vineyard; thus here too, when they were not willing to be present at the marriage, then He called others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then could be more ungrateful than they, when being bidden to a marriage they rush away?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who would not choose to come to a marriage, and that a King’s marriage, and of a King making a marriage for a Son?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore is it called a marriage? one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That thou mightest learn God’s tender care, His yearning towards us, the cheerfulness of the state of things, that there is nothing sorrowful there, nor sad, but all things are full of spiritual joy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also John calls Him a bridegroom, therefore Paul again saith, “For I have espoused you to one husband;”and, “This is a great mystery, but I speak concerning Christ and the Church.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then is not the bride said to be espoused to Him, but to the Son?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because she that is espoused to the Son, is espoused to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is indifferent in Scripture that the one or the other should be said, because of the identityof the substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hereby He proclaimed the resurrection also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since in what went before He had spoken of the death, He shows that even after the death, then is the marriage, then the bridegroom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not even so do these become better men nor more gentle, than which what can be worse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this again is a third accusation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The first that they killed the prophets; then the son; afterwards that even when they had slain Him, and were bidden unto the marriage of Him that was slain, by the very one that was slain, they come not, but feign excuses, yokes of oxen, and pieces of ground, and wives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet the excuses seem to be reasonable; but hence we learn, though the things which hinder us be necessary, to set the things spiritual at a higher price than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He not suddenly, but a long time before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Tell,” He saith, “them that are bidden;” and again, “Call them that were bidden;” which circumstance makes the charge against them heavier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when were they bidden?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By all the prophets; by John again; for unto Christ he would pass all on, saying, “He must increase, I must decrease;”by the Son Himself again, “Come unto me, all ye that labor and are heavy laden, and I will refresh you;”and again, “If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not by words only, but also by actions did He bid them, after His ascension by Peter, and those with him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For He that wrought effectually in Peter,” it is said, “to the apostleship of the circumcision, was mighty also in me towards the Gentiles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since on seeing the Son, they were wroth and slew Him, He bids them again by His servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And unto what doth He bid them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Unto labors, and toils, and sweat?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay but unto pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “My oxen,” He saith, “and my fatlings are killed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how complete His banquet,how great His munificence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even this shamed them, but the more long-suffering He showed, so much the more were they hardened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not for press of business, but from “making light of it,” they did not come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “How then do some bring forward marriages, others yokes of oxen? these things surely are of want of leisure.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, for when spiritual things call us, there is no press of business that has the power of necessity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to me they seem moreover to make use of these excuses, putting forward these things as cloke for their negligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not this only is the grievous thing, that they came not, but also that which is a far more violent and furious act, to have even beaten them that came, and to have used them despitefully, and to have slain them; this is worse than the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For those others came, demanding produce and fruits, and were slain; but these, bidding them to the marriage of Him that had been slain by them, and these again are murdered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is equal to this madness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This Paul also was laying to their charge, when he said, “Who both killed the Lord, and their own prophets, and have persecuted us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Moreover, that they may not say, “He is an adversary of God, and therefore we do not come,” hear what they say who are bidding them; that it is the father who is making the marriage, and that it is He who is bidding them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then did He after these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since they were not willing to come, yea and also slew those that came unto them; He burns up their cities, and sent His armies and slew them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things He saith, declaring beforehand the things that took place under Vespasian and Titus, and that they provoked the father also, by not believing in Him; it is the father at any rate who was avenging.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for this reason let me add, not straightway after Christ was slain did the capture take place, but after forty years, that He might show His long suffering, when they had slain Stephen, when they had put James to death, when they had spitefully entreated the apostles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the truth of the event, and its quickness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while John was yet living, and many other of them that were with Christ, these things came to pass, and they that had heard these words were witnesses of the events.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See then care utterable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He had planted a vineyard; He had done all things, and finished; when His servants had been put to death, He sent other servants; when those had been slain, He sent the son; and when He was put to death, He bids them to the marriage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They would not come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this He sends other servants, and they slew these also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then upon this He slays them, as being incurably diseased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that they were incurably diseased, was proved not by their acts only, but by the fact, that even when harlots and publicans had believed, they did these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that, not by their own crimes alone, but also from what others were able to do aright, these men are condemned,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any one should say, that not then were they out of the Gentiles called, I mean, when the apostles had been beaten and had suffered ten thousand things, but straightway after the resurrection (for then He said to them, “Go ye and make disciples of all nations.”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We would say, that both before the crucifixion, and after the crucifixion, they addressed themselves to them first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both before the crucifixion, He saith to them, “Go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel;”and after the crucifixion, so far from forbidding, He even commanded them to address themselves to the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though He said, “Make disciples of all nations,” yet when on the point of ascending into Heaven, He declared that unto those first they were to address themselves; For, “ye shall receive power,” saith He, “after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you, and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judæa, and unto the uttermost part of the earth;”and Paul again, “He that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, was mighty in me also toward the Gentiles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore the apostles also went first unto the Jews, and when they had tarried a long time in Jerusalem, and then had been driven away by them, in this way they were scattered abroad unto the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see thou even herein His bounty; “As many as ye shall find,” saith He, “bid to the marriage.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For before this, as I said, they addressed themselves both to Jews and Greeks, tarrying for the most part in Judæa; but since they continued to lay plots against them, hear Paul interpreting this parable, and saying thus, “It was necessary that the word of God should first have been spoken to you, but since ye judge yourselves unworthy, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore Christ also saith, “The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden were not worthy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He knew this indeed even before, but that He might leave them no pretext of a shameless sort of contradiction, although He knew it, to them first He both came and sent, both stopping their mouths, and teaching us to fulfill all our parts, though no one should derive any profit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since then they were not worthy, go ye, saith He, into the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid; both the common sort, and the outcasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because He had said in every way,“The harlots and publicans shall inherit heaven;” and, “The first shall be last, and the last first;” He shows that justly do these things come to pass; which more than anything stung the Jews, and goaded them far more grievously than their overthrow, to see those from the Gentiles brought into their privileges, and into far greater than theirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then in order that not even these should put confidence in their faith alone, He discourses unto them also concerning the judgment to be passed upon wicked actions; to them that have not yet believed, of coming unto Him by faith, and to them that have believed, of care with respect to their life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the garment is life and practice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet the calling was of grace; wherefore then doth He take a strict account?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because although to be called and to be cleansed was of grace, yet, when called and clothed in clean garments, to continue keeping them so, this is of the diligence of them that are called.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The being called was not of merit, but of grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was fit therefore to make a return for the grace, and not to show forth such great wickedness after the honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But I have not enjoyed,” one may say, “so much advantage as the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, but thou hast enjoyed far greater benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what things were being prepared for them throughout all their time, these thou hast received at once, not being worthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also saith, “And that the Gentiles might glorify God for His mercy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what things were due to them, these thou hast received.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also great is the punishment appointed for them that have been remiss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as they did despite by not coming, so also thou by thus sitting down with a corrupt life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to come in with filthy garments is this namely, to depart hence having one’s life impure; wherefore also he was speechless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how, although the fact was so manifest, He doth not punish at once, until he himself, who has sinned, has passed the sentence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by having nothing to reply he condemned himself, and so is taken away to the unutterable torments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For do not now, on hearing of darkness, suppose he is punished by this, by sending into a place where there is no light only, but where “there is” also “weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He saith, indicating the intolerable pains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear ye, as many as having partaken of the mysteries, and having been present at the marriage, clothe your souls with filthy deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear whence ye were called.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From the highway.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lame and halt in soul, which is a much more grievous thing than the mutilation of the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Reverence the love of Him, who called you, and let no one continue to have filthy garments, but let each of you busy himself about the clothing of your soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear, ye women; hear, ye men; we need not these garments that are bespangled with gold, that adorn our outward parts,but those others, that adorn the inward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whilst we have these former, it is difficult to put on those latter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not possible at the same time to deck both soul and body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not possible at the same time both to serve mammon, and to obey Christ as we ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us put off us therefore this grievous tyranny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither if any one were to adorn thy house by hanging it with golden curtains, and were to make thee sit there in rags, naked, wouldest thou endure it with meekness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But lo, now thou doest this to thyself, decking the house of thy soul, I mean the body, with curtains beyond number, but leaving the soul itself to sit in rags.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowest thou not that the king ought to be adorned more than the city? so therefore while for the city hangings are prepared of linen, for the king there is a purple robe and a diadem.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so do thou wrap the body with a much meaner dress, but the mind do thou clothe in purple, and put a crown on it, and set it on a high and conspicuous chariot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For now thou art doing the opposite, decking the city in various ways, but suffering the king, the mind, to be dragged bound after the brute passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Rememberest thou not, that thou art bidden to a marriage, and to God’s marriage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Considerest thou not how the soul that is bidden ought to enter into those chambers, clad, and decked with fringes of gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou that I show thee them that are clad thus, them that have on a marriage garment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Call to mind those holy persons, of whom I discoursed to you of late, them that wear garments of hair, them that dwell in the deserts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These above all are the wearers of the garments of that wedding; this is evident from hence, that how many soever purple robes thou wert to give them, they would not choose to receive them; but much as a king, if any one were to take the beggar’s rags, and exhort him to put them on, would abhor the clothing, so would those persons also his purple robe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And from no other cause have they this feeling, but because of knowing the beauty of their own raiment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore even that purple robe they spurn like the spider’s web.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these things hath their sackcloth taught them; for indeed they are far more exalted and more glorious than the very king who reigns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou wert able to open the doors of the mind, and to look upon their soul, and all their ornaments within, surely thou wouldest fall down upon the earth, not bearing the glory of their beauty, and the splendor of those garments, and the lightning brightness of their conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we could tell also of men of old, great and to be admired; but since visible examples lead on more those of grosser souls, therefore do I send you even to the tabernacles of those holy persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they have nothing sorrowful, but as if in heaven they had pitched their tents, even so are they encamped far off the wearisome things of this present life, in campaign against the devils; and as in choirs, so do they war against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore I say, they have fixed their tents, and have fled from cities, and markets, and houses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that warreth cannot sit in a house, but he must make his habitation of a temporary kind, as on the point of removing straightway, and so dwell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such are all those persons, contrary to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we indeed live not as in a camp, but as in a city at peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who in a camp ever lays foundation, and builds himself a house, which he is soon after to leave?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is not one; but should any one attempt it, he is put to death as a traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who in a camp buys acres of land, and makes for himself trades?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is not one, and very reasonably.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For thou art come here,” they would say, “to fight, not to traffic; why then dost thou trouble thyself about the place, which in a little time thou wilt leave?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When we are gone away to our country, do these things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The same do I now say to thee also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When we have removed to the city that is above, do these things: or rather thou wilt have no need of labors there; after that the king will do all things for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But here it is enough to dig a ditch round only, and to fix a palisade, but of building houses there is no need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear what was the life of the Scythians, that lived in their wagons, such, as they say, are the habits of the shepherd tribes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So ought Christians to live; to go about the world, warring against the devil, rescuing the captives held in subjection by him, and to be in freedom from all worldly things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why preparest thou a house, O man, that thou mayest bind thyself more?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou bury a treasure, and invite the enemy against thyself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou compass thyself with walls, and prepare a prison for thyself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if these things seem to thee to be hard, let us go away unto the tents of those men, that by their deeds we may learn the easiness thereof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they having set up huts, if they must depart from these, depart like as soldiers, having left their camp in peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so likewise are they encamped, or rather even much more beautifully.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it is more pleasant to behold a desert containing huts of monks in close succession, than soldiers stretching the canvas in a camp, and fixing spears, and suspending from the point of the spears saffron garments,and a multitude of men having heads of brass, and the bosses of the shields glistening much, and men armed all throughout with steel, and royal courts hastily made, and ground levelled far, and men dining and piping.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is this spectacle so delightful as that of which I now speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we were to go away into the wilderness, and look at the tents of Christ’s soldiers, we shall see not canvas stretched, neither points of spears, nor golden garments making a royal pavilion; but like as if any one upon an earth much larger than this earth, yea infinite, had stretched out many heavens, strange and awful would be the sight he showed; even so may one see here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in nothing are their lodging-places in a condition inferior to the heavens; for the angels lodge with them, and the Lord of the angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they came to Abraham, a man having a wife, and bringing up children, because they saw him hospitable; when they find much more abundant virtue, and a man delivered from the body, and in the flesh disregarding the flesh, much more do they tarry there, and celebrate the choral feast that becomes them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is moreover a table amongst them pure from all covetousness, and full of self-denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No streams of blood are amongst them, nor cutting up of flesh, nor heaviness of head, nor dainty cooking, neither are there unpleasing smells of meat amongst them, nor disagreeable smoke, neither runnings and tumults, and disturbances, and wearisome clamors; but bread and water, the latter from a pure fountain, the former from honest labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any time they should be minded to feast more sumptuously, their sumptuousness consists of fruits, and greater is the pleasure there than at royal tables.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no fear there, or trembling; no ruler accuses, no wife provokes, no child casts into sadness, no dis orderly mirth dissipates, no multitude of flatterers puffs up; but the table is an angel’s table free from all such turmoil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for a couch they have grass only beneath them, like as Christ did when making a dinner in the wilderness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And many of them do this, not being even under shelter, but for a roof they have heaven, and the moon instead of the light of a candle, not wanting oil, nor one to attend to it; on them alone does it shine worthily from on high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This table even angels from heaven beholding are delighted and pleased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if over one sinner that repenteth they rejoice, over so many just men imitating them, what will they not do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There are not master and slave; all are slaves, all free men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And do not think the saying to be a dark proverb, for they are indeed slaves one of another, and masters one of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They have no occasion to be in sadness when evening has overtaken them, as many men feel, revolving the anxious thoughts that spring from the evils of the day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They have no occasion after their supper to be careful about robbers, and to shut the doors, and to put bars against them, neither to dread the other ills, of which many are afraid, extinguishing their candles with strict care, lest a spark anywhere should set the house on fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And their conversation again is full of the same calm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they talk not of these things, whereof we discourse, that are nothing to us; such a one is made governor, such a one has ceased to be governor; such a one is dead, and another has succeeded to the inheritance, and all such like, but always about the things to come do they speak and seek wisdom; and as though dwelling in another world, as though they had migrated unto heaven itself, as living there, even so all their conversation is about the things there, about Abraham’s bosom, about the crowns of the saints, about the choiring with Christ; and of things present they have neither any memory nor thought, but like as we should not deign to speak at all of what the ants do in their holes and clefts; so neither do they of what we do; but about the King that is above, about the war in which they are engaged, about the devil’s crafts, about the good deeds which the saints have achieved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherein therefore are we different from ants, when compared with them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as they care for the things of the body, so also do we; and would it were for these alone: but now it is even for things far worse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not for necessary things only do we care like them, but also for things superfluous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For those insects pursue a business free from all blame, but we follow after all covetousness, and not even the ways of ants do we imitate, but the ways of wolves, but the ways of leopards, or rather we are even worse than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to them nature has assigned that they should be thus fed, but us God hath honored with speech, and a sense of equity,and we are become worse than the wild beasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas we are worse than the brutes, those men are equal to the angels, being strangers and pilgrims as to the things here; and all things in them are made different from us, clothing, and food, and house, and shoes, and speech.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if any one were to hear them conversing and us, then he would know full well, how they indeed are citizens of heaven, but we are not worthy so much as of the earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that therefore, when any one invested with rank is come unto them, then is all inflated pride found utterly vain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the laborer there, and he that hath no experience of worldly affairs, sits near him that is a commander of troops, and prides himself on his authority, upon the grass, upon a mean cushion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there are none to extol him, none to puff him up; but the same result takes place, as if any one were to go to a goldsmith, and a garden of roses, for he receives some brightness from the gold and from the roses; so they too, gaining a little from the splendor of these, are delivered from their former arrogance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as if any were to go upon a high place, though he be exceedingly short, he appears high; so these too, coming unto their exalted minds, appear like them, so long as they abide there, but when they are gone down are abased again, on descending from that height.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A king is nothing amongst them, a governor is nothing; but like as we, when children are playing at these things, laugh; so do they also utterly spurn the inflamed pride of them who strut without.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is evident from hence, that if any one would give them a kingdom to possess in security, they would never take it; yet they would take it, unless their thoughts were upon what is greater than it, unless they accounted the thing to be but for a season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shall we not go over unto blessedness so great?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shall we not come unto these angels; shall we not receive clean garments, and join in the ceremonies of this wedding feast; but shall we continue begging, in no respect in a better condition than the poor in the streets, or rather in a state far worse and more wretched?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For much worse than these are they that are rich in evil ways, and it is better to beg than to spoil, for the one hath excuse, but the other brings punishment; and the beggar in no degree offends God, but this other both men and God; and undergoes the labors of rapine, but all the enjoyment thereof other men often reap.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowing then these things, let us lay aside all covetousness, and covet the things above, with great earnestness “taking the kingdom by force.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it cannot be, it cannot be that any one who is remiss should enter therein.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But God grant that we all having become earnest, and watchful may attain thereto, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might, world without end.
